subject: Science
chapter: The Amazing World of Solute, Solvent, and Solutions

key_points: A solution is formed when a solute dissolves completely in a solvent
The solvent is usually present in larger quantity than the solute
Solutions are homogeneous mixtures with uniform composition
Solubility refers to the maximum amount of solute that can dissolve in a solvent
Temperature and stirring can affect the rate of dissolving
examples: Salt water is a solution where salt is the solute and water is the solvent
Sugar syrup is formed when sugar dissolves in water
Air is a gaseous solution consisting mainly of nitrogen and oxygen
Carbonated drinks contain dissolved carbon dioxide
Sea water contains dissolved salts and minerals
formulas_or_processes: Solubility: Maximum amount of solute that dissolves in a given solvent
Dissolution: Process of solute particles spreading uniformly in solvent
Saturated solution: Solution that cannot dissolve more solute
Unsaturated solution: Solution that can dissolve more solute
exam_tips: Understand the difference between saturated and unsaturated solutions
Practice identifying solute and solvent in different examples
Remember that solutions have uniform composition throughout
